channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Curated geopolitical news headlines from Tier 1 sources

item.title: US Navy intercepts Iranian attacks on three warships in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The US military said it intercepted Iranian missile, drone, and small boat attacks targeting three Navy destroyers in the Strait of Hormuz, as tensions threaten the month-old ceasefire between Washington and Tehran.

item.title: Chinese-owned oil tanker attacked near Strait of Hormuz
item.description: A Chinese-owned oil products tanker was attacked near the Strait of Hormuz on Monday, according to Chinese media, as the US launched and then suspended a plan to help stranded vessels transiting the vital waterway.

item.title: US carries out retaliatory strikes on Iran after Hormuz clash
item.description: The US military conducted retaliatory strikes on Iranian targets it said were responsible for attacking American forces, calling the hostilities "unprovoked" amid renewed fighting in the Gulf region.

item.title: China sees weakened US deterrence on Taiwan as Iran war drains weapons
item.description: Chinese analysts say America's ability to deter a war over Taiwan is weakened by the Iran conflict, giving Beijing leverage ahead of President Trump's upcoming summit with Xi Jinping.

item.title: China pushes Trump for concessions on Taiwan ahead of Beijing summit
item.description: China is pressing for US policy concessions on Taiwan as President Trump prepares to visit Beijing, seeking to capitalize on American preoccupation with the Iran war and stretched military resources.

item.title: Ukraine-Sweden Gripen Fighter Deal Could Be Signed Within Months
item.description: Ukraine's defense minister says talks for Saab-made Gripen fighter jets are progressing well, with a potential deal signed within months during his visit to Stockholm.

item.title: North Korea Deploys New Artillery Systems Capable of Striking Seoul
item.description: Kim Jong Un inspected new long-range artillery with ranges exceeding 60km to be deployed at the South Korean border, bolstering ability to hit Seoul, state media reports.

item.title: $7 Billion in Well-Timed Oil Bets Before Iran War News Sparks Scrutiny
item.description: Legal experts and lawmakers are calling for investigations into whether well-timed oil trades before the Iran war news were based on inside information or leaks.

item.title: IOC Lifts All Restrictions on Belarusian Athletes
item.description: The International Olympic Committee removed all restrictions on Belarusian athletes, clearing them for international competitions including LA 2028 Olympic qualifiers.

item.title: Vance and Rubio Take Center Stage as 2028 Republican Contenders
item.description: The Vice President and Secretary of State offered contrasting styles this week, signaling early positioning for a potential post-Trump Republican future.

item.title: UK Labour Suffers Heavy Losses as Reform UK Makes Gains
item.description: Partial results from English local elections show large losses for Prime Minister Keir Starmer's Labour Party and gains for the hard-right Reform UK party.

item.title: British MPs to Visit China for First Time in Seven Years
item.description: A cross-party delegation of UK lawmakers will visit China this month for the first time since 2019, signaling warming ties following Prime Minister Keir Starmer's January visit.

item.title: Trump Announces Withdrawal of 5,000 US Troops from Germany
item.description: President Trump said he will withdraw 5,000 U.S. troops from Germany as tensions with NATO allies widen over the Iran war, raising questions about the future of the transatlantic alliance.

item.title: China Export Growth Quickens on Iran War Stockpiling
item.description: China's export growth likely accelerated in April as companies rushed to stockpile components amid fears the Iran war could push input costs higher, a Reuters poll shows.

item.title: ASEAN Leaders Meet on Iran War Economic Fallout
item.description: Southeast Asian leaders are meeting under pressure to mitigate the impact on their economies from the Iran war, with one minister saying it "should not have occurred in the first place."

item.title: Toyota Forecasts 20% Profit Drop as Iran War Disrupts Supply Chains
item.description: Toyota halved its quarterly profit and forecast a 20% annual decline as supply chain disruptions from the Iran war and rising costs weigh on the world's largest automaker.

item.title: Chinese-Owned Oil Tanker Attacked Near Strait of Hormuz
item.description: A Chinese-owned oil products tanker was attacked near the Strait of Hormuz, with the chief engineer reporting it remains unclear who fired on the vessel amid ongoing regional tensions.

item.title: Pakistan Seeks Singapore's Help for Return of Seafarers From US-Seized Vessels
item.description: Pakistan's foreign minister requested Singapore's assistance in repatriating Pakistani and Iranian seafarers aboard vessels seized by the US amid the Iran conflict.

item.title: Israel Opposition Bloc Could Secure 61 Seats Under Three-Party Merger, Poll Shows
item.description: A new poll suggests a merger between Bennett, Eisenkot, and Liberman's parties could form a 61-seat opposition bloc without relying on Arab parties, challenging Netanyahu's coalition.

item.title: Russia and Ukraine accuse each other of violating Victory Day ceasefire
item.description: Russia and Ukraine traded accusations of violating a two-day ceasefire announced by Moscow to cover Soviet Victory Day celebrations. The fragile truce failed to hold as both sides reported attacks along the frontlines.

item.title: Paraguay reaffirms Taiwan ties as China pressures last South American ally
item.description: Paraguay President Santiago Pena visited Taipei to reaffirm diplomatic support for Taiwan, one of only 12 countries maintaining formal relations. China has intensified efforts to draw Paraguay away, with lawmakers visiting Beijing amid internal debate over economic costs.

item.title: Armenia distances from Russia as Pashinyan says "not an ally" on Ukraine
item.description: Armenian PM Nikol Pashinyan clarified Armenia is not allied with Russia on Ukraine, confirming he will skip Moscow Victory Day parade. The Kremlin summoned Armenia's ambassador after Zelensky's visit to Yerevan, showing tensions as Armenia drifts toward EU.

item.title: Maersk warns energy crunch to persist even if Iran peace deal struck
item.description: Shipping giant Maersk said the Iran war has pushed fuel costs up nearly $500 million a month, with the energy crisis set to continue even if a peace deal is reached. The warning came as oil supplies tighten further with depleted storage tanks meeting peak summer demand.

item.title: US and Iran Exchange Fire as Fragile Ceasefire Shows Cracks
item.description: US military carried out retaliatory strikes on Iranian targets after renewed hostilities in the Strait of Hormuz. The UAE reported intercepting drones and missiles as Trump insisted the ceasefire still holds.

item.title: Netanyahu Vows No Immunity for Enemies After Beirut Strike
item.description: Israel struck a Hezbollah commander in Beirut, its first strike on the capital since the ceasefire. Netanyahu declared there would be no immunity for militants threatening Israel.
